element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Direct control and individual ant manipulation established immediately
Primary communication mechanic for directing ant colony behavior
Resource collection and trophallaxis (regurgitated food exchange)
Competitive presence; defeat is primary victory condition
Spatial control and strategic colony layout
Yellow ant and recruits attack enemy ants and small predators
Require group tactics; yellow ant plus recruited workers to defeat
Environmental threats; rain erases pheromone trails and floods nests
Most severe hazard; human intervention threat
Colony expansion to multiple map squares through reproduction
Expansion into human dwelling; proximity to human owners
Final victory condition; ultimate strategic goal

typical_relief_triggers: Red ant colony defeated in a zone
Natural hazard passes (rain stops, footstep ends)
typical_tension_triggers: Pheromone trail erasure by environmental damage
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Naturalistic cyclical rhythm with escalating complexity. Early phases emphasize resource gathering and colony building with low-threat encounters. Mid-game introduces competitive pressure from red ant colonies and environmental hazards. Late-game transitions to strategic expansion across multiple zones with compound threats. Full Game spans longer cycle than Quick Game, promoting emerging complexity from simple rule interactions.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Underground side-view tunnels represent optional deeper expansion; surface overhead view shows mandatory territorial spread

spatial_structure_detail.overview_perspective: Top-down overhead view of yard and house exterior (Quick/Full Game surface)
spatial_structure_detail.underground_perspective: Side-view cross-section of ant colony tunnels and chambers
spatial_structure_detail.dual_layer_system: Player switches between surface yard activities and underground nest management; surface coordinates map to underground network locations
spatial_structure_detail.cardinal_movement: Omnidirectional movement across yard in overhead view; vertical and horizontal tunneling underground
spatial_structure_detail.zone_progression: Quick Game: single yard patch. Full Game: map grid of squares representing yard areas and house interior; player must conquer or occupy each square by establishing satellite colonies
spatial_structure_detail.spatial_hazard_placement: Electrical outlets, food sources, water hazards distributed throughout yard; house interior represents densest human-controlled zone

difficulty_curve.early_game: Low threat; player learns pheromone mechanics, ant control, basic food gathering with minimal enemy pressure or hazards
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate threat escalation; red ant colonies become aggressive; first encounters with large predators and natural hazards increase; player must coordinate multi-ant tactics
difficulty_curve.late_game: High sustained pressure; multiple hazard types active simultaneously; large-scale territorial conflicts; Full Game requires strategic zone conquest and colony expansion across house interior
notable_spikes: First red ant colony combat encounter
First large predator (spider/antlion/caterpillar) encounter requiring group tactics
Environmental hazard cascade (multiple hazard types active in sequence)
Full Game transition to house interior with human owner presence
Final push to eliminate all red colonies and drive humans from house

notable_level_patterns.description: Quick Game format: single yard patch becomes contested arena between black and red ant colonies. Victory requires eliminating enemy colony through direct combat, pheromone-led resource control, or attrition.
Full Game format: player must establish satellite colonies in adjacent map squares by producing queens and drones. Each zone presents different hazard profiles and red ant presence levels.

SNES scenarios add environmental hazards specific to locale (lawnmower frequency, pesticide application timing, water proximity). Player must navigate hazard patterns while maintaining colony viability.
Large predators (spiders, antlions, caterpillars) require emergent swarm tactics. Single yellow ant cannot defeat; must recruit and coordinate group attacks. Creates dynamic difficulty spikes.

data_quality_notes: T3 tier extraction. Source provides gameplay mechanics overview but lacks detailed level sequencing, precise hazard introduction timing, or quantified tension/relief metrics. Rhythm and spatial patterns inferred from described game systems and mode structures.
